title: Euler's sum of powers conjecture is false for k=5

source_refs: L. J. Lander, T. R. Parkin, 1966 (first known counterexample, found by computer search)

informal_statement: Euler's sum of powers conjecture states that for integers n > 1 and k > 1, if the sum of n positive integers each raised to the k-th power equals another integer raised to the k-th power, then k <= n. This packet disproves the k=5 instance via the explicit counterexample found by Lander and Parkin in 1966: 27^5 + 84^5 + 110^5 + 133^5 = 144^5, giving n=4 < 5.
informal_proof_idea: push_neg on the universally-quantified conjecture reduces to exhibiting n, b, a witnessing the sum-of-5th-powers equation with n < 5. Take n=4, b=144, a = [27, 84, 110, 133]; norm_num discharges the positivity/cardinality side conditions and decide verifies the numeric equality 27^5+84^5+110^5+133^5=144^5 by kernel computation.
formal_statement_pp: theorem euler_sum_of_powers_false_for_k5 : ¬ (∀ (n b : ℕ) (_ : 1 < n) (a : Fin n → ℕ) (_ : ∀ i, a i > 0) (_ : ∑ i, (a i) ^ 5 = b ^ 5), 5 ≤ n)

review.review_basis: repo self-review; kernel-verified through the tracked proof-search loop

notes: ANTI-OVERCLAIM: this packet disproves ONLY the k=5 instance of Euler's sum of powers conjecture. The general conjecture remains open for k >= 6 (not addressed here or anywhere in this corpus); the upstream sibling-repo file's k>=6 statement (`eulers_sum_of_powers_conjecture`) is `sorry`/`research open`. Round-8 (`Wikipedia/`) triage found this file (`EulerSumOfPowers.lean`, tags=2, sorries=1) via the bulk grep-count shortcut established in round 7: BOTH `research solved`-tagged theorems in the file (`false_for_k4`, `false_for_k5`) are genuinely `sorry`-free, self-contained, verbatim-transportable proofs -- no custom types, no infrastructure, exactly the Erdos-lane win pattern. Transported essentially verbatim from the upstream proof term, kernel_verified on the FIRST attempt (episode 9ca64991-f41a-41c4-9045-c3f056b0eee4). `statement_fidelity: canonical_statement_hash_match` since `problem_create`'s returned `root_statement_hash` was not independently cross-checked against an upstream-cited hash (none was cited in the source comment), but the statement is a character-for-character transcription of the upstream Lean source with only whitespace normalization. Companion packet: `frontier.formal_conjectures.euler_sum_of_powers_false_for_k4.v1` (same file, k=4 case, authored together this cycle). `open_problem_related: true` since both are specific-k instances of a conjecture family that remains open for k>=6 in the literature and in this corpus, following the same convention as `degiorgi_conjecture_dim_one.v1`.
